title: I stopped wearing a watch
summary: A blog post about why the writer no longer wears a watch — and what happened.
body:
Last year, I stopped wearing a watch. I did not plan it. My old watch was broken, and I did not buy a new one. At first, it felt strange. I looked at my wrist many times a day. Every time, I saw only my skin. I felt a little lost.

Then something changed. I started to look at the sky more. I looked at people's faces. I asked friends and shop workers, 'Excuse me, what time is it?' Most of them smiled. One man said, 'You don't have a phone?' Yes, I have a phone. But the phone shows ten other things too. I did not want ten things. I only wanted the time.

After a few weeks, I noticed I was rarely late. I do not understand why. Maybe because I now ask people the time, I think about time more carefully. Or maybe I am just lucky.

My friends say, 'You should buy a watch again. It is easier.' Maybe they are right. But for now, I like the empty wrist. It feels light. It feels open. The day is not a list of minutes any more. It is just a day.

grammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Rarely' (hiếm khi) đứng trước động từ chính: I was rarely late. Khác: always, usually, often, sometimes, never.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Adverbs of frequency (rarely, often, usually, never) come before the main verb: 'I was rarely late.'

blanks.explanation: 'I started to look at the sky more.'
'The phone shows ten other things too.'
He noticed he was rarely late.
His friends advise: 'You should buy a watch again.'

translationVi:
Năm ngoái, tôi ngừng đeo đồng hồ. Tôi không có dự định gì cả. Đồng hồ cũ của tôi bị hỏng, và tôi không mua cái mới. Lúc đầu cảm giác lạ lắm. Tôi nhìn cổ tay rất nhiều lần trong ngày. Lần nào cũng chỉ thấy da của mình. Tôi cảm thấy hơi lạc lõng.

Rồi có một điều thay đổi. Tôi bắt đầu nhìn bầu trời nhiều hơn. Tôi nhìn khuôn mặt người ta. Tôi hỏi bạn bè và người bán hàng: 'Xin lỗi, mấy giờ rồi ạ?' Phần lớn họ mỉm cười. Một ông nói: 'Cậu không có điện thoại à?' Có, tôi có điện thoại. Nhưng điện thoại còn cho xem mười thứ khác. Tôi không muốn mười thứ. Tôi chỉ muốn giờ.

Sau vài tuần, tôi nhận ra mình hiếm khi đi trễ. Tôi không biết tại sao. Có thể vì giờ tôi phải hỏi giờ, nên tôi nghĩ về thời gian kỹ hơn. Hoặc có thể tôi chỉ may mắn.

Bạn tôi bảo: 'Cậu nên mua đồng hồ lại đi. Tiện hơn.' Có thể họ đúng. Nhưng giờ tôi thích cái cổ tay trống. Nhẹ. Thoáng. Một ngày không còn là một danh sách các phút nữa. Chỉ là một ngày thôi.

questions.prompt: Why did the writer stop wearing a watch?
options: His watch broke, and he did not buy a new one
questions.explanation: 'My old watch was broken, and I did not buy a new one.'

questions.prompt: How did the writer feel at first?
questions.hint: Look at the end of paragraph 1.

questions.prompt: What did he start looking at more?
questions.explanation: 'I started to look at the sky more. I looked at people's faces.'

questions.prompt: Why doesn't he use his phone for the time?
options: The phone shows ten other things too
questions.explanation: 'But the phone shows ten other things too.'

questions.prompt: What changed about being on time?
Why might he be on time now?
options: Asking others makes him think about time more carefully
questions.explanation: '…I now ask people the time, I think about time more carefully.'

questions.prompt: Does the writer agree with them?
options: Maybe — for now he likes the empty wrist
questions.explanation: 'Maybe they are right. But for now, I like the empty wrist.'

questions.prompt: How does he describe his wrist now?

options: Removing a small object can change how you feel about time
questions.explanation: Stopping the small habit changed his experience of the day.
questions.hint: Think about what changed in him.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The writer says 'the day is not a list of minutes any more. It is just a day.' What does measuring something carefully — time, food, steps, sleep — give us, and what does it take away?
